articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

Goal! FC Augsburg 0, Borussia Dortmund 1. Donyell Malen (Borussia Dortmund) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Serhou Guirassy with a through ball.
Goal! FC Augsburg 1, Borussia Dortmund 1. Alexis Claude-Maurice (FC Augsburg) right footed shot from outside the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Marius Wolf following a fast break.
Dimitris Giannoulis (FC Augsburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Emre Can replaces Waldemar Anton because of an injury.

Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Maximilian Beier replaces Felix Nmecha.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Almugera Kabar replaces Julian Ryerson because of an injury.
Almugera Kabar (Borussia Dortmund) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Phillip Tietz (FC Augsburg) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Samuel Essende replaces Phillip Tietz.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Henri Koudossou replaces Marius Wolf because of an injury.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Maximilian Bauer replaces Chrislain Matsima because of an injury.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Arne Maier replaces Frank Onyeka because of an injury.

Jeffrey Gouweleeuw (FC Augsburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Cole Campbell replaces Donyell Malen.
Ramy Bensebaini (Borussia Dortmund) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Second yellow card to Almugera Kabar (Borussia Dortmund) for a bad foul.
Second Half ends, FC Augsburg 2, Borussia Dortmund 1.

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (FC Augsburg vs. Borussia Dortmund - Game Highlights) 2024/10/26 ESHEET

article.story:
Borussia Dortmund slumped to a 2-1 defeat at Augsburg in the Bundesliga on Saturday and were left with 10 men as they suffered a second defeat in five days.

Alexis Claude-Maurice struck twice for Augsburg as Dortmund lost again after Tuesday's 5-2 reverse away to Real Madrid after wasting a 2-0 lead in the Champions League.

Dortmund, Champions League finalists last season, have not won any of their four away games in the Bundesliga this season and remain in seventh place on 13 points after two defeats and a win in their last three league games.

The latest loss also piles the pressure on coach Nuri Sahin, in his first season in charge, after he was heavily criticised for his team wasting their 2-0 lead over Madrid and conceding five goals in the second half.

"Eighty percent possession does not necessarily mean more chances. We just did not get into the danger area often enough," Sahin told a news conference.

Dortmund had more than 70% possession throughout but hardly had any scoring opportunities for more than an hour after their 1-0 lead in the fourth minute.

"[The] fact is for 80% possession we had far too few chances. We need to work on creating more chances," Sahin said. "Our squad is not without experience and I expect all of us together, players and coaches, to face this current situation and go through it together."

RB Leipzig provisionally took over top spot on 20 points with a 3-1 home victory over Freiburg. Bayern Munich, who are in second spot on 17, are in action at VfL Bochum on Sunday.

Dortmund had the perfect start as they struck on the break with Donyell Malen slotting in for a fourth-minute lead.

But there was little else that came from the Dortmund attack in the first half and Augsburg got an unexpected equaliser in the 25th when Claude-Maurice broke through, found no resistance from four players and lots of space to drill in from 25 metres.

The Frenchman, making his first league start for Augsburg, netted a second to complete the comeback five minutes after the restart, curling a low shot home from the edge of the box.

Dortmund, who are in action in the German Cup against VfL Wolfsburg next week, had substitute Almugera Kabar sent off for a second booking on his league debut in stoppage time.

They also had to take off Waldemar Anton and Julian Ryerson due to injuries.
